channel.title: Comments for page "We Have Dismissed That Claim"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "We Have Dismissed That Claim"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: http://www.scp-wiki.net/bargain-bin-of-direct-to-forum-sequels

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Late as fuck reply, but where's the God damn sequel?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love me a good historiographical analysis! It's very reminiscent of the comparison between how historians and archaeologists approached their fields a hundred years ago, and how they approach them now. Dr. Von Schmidt is like those scholars of the past, who had a theory and interpreted the evidence in such a way as to suit their theory, ignoring or dismissing everything that was problematic. Doctor Rex is like those scholars of the modern age, coming along and poking holes in old theories, and while Rex and everyone else who studies the past may not be able to definitively prove a different theory, that's the norm for their fields of work.
Well thought out, well written, and if I could give a second +1 I'd do it in a heartbeat.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A late note on dialogue tags.

I've corrected a number of errors, but also: Stuff like "he spat" and "she scoffed" should be cut entirely. Even stuff like "retorted" is iffy because it's implied already; if it's not implied, then sure, go for it, but in most cases here it IS implied. Just use "said" or "asked" or whatever.

Same goes for stuff like:

He sighed exasperatedly.

We can tell the sigh is an exasperated sigh. We don't need the adverb. That's a younger reader thing.

Same for:

Rex was unimpressed.
the General asked incredulously.

I mean, ouch.

Otherwise, I don't know what to say about this conceptually, because I'm too distracted by how hilariously over the top the dialogue is. If you try speaking the dialogue aloud, you can practically hear the MST3K guys in the background. And there's way too many cliches, as well, right down to the "I hope you're right…" repeated ending lines.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I know this one's old, but I believe the comment deserves nothing less than over NINE THOUSAND +s. XD

Btw, was there a Sword of Shannara reference mixed in there? =D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Beautiful. You have to figure that the Foundation has equal amounts of people support scientific, magical, etc causes behind things. Arbitrary skepticism goes all kinds of directions and everyone has their personal hobby horse to ride.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That it is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
General, when was the last time you heard of a spearman beating a tank in combat?

I really hope this is a Civilization reference.
Anyway, +1!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: dear lord, this is even worse than the last one D:… i already vote for the last comment, and this is something i need to forget. if you excuse me i will crush my head against a wall

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: you should say sorry but this thing… +1 to your comment

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really? I was under the impression that victorian england was just like the modern era, except that everyone wore hats and spoke funny. Who knew?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh damn it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wish I could upvote comments, and now that song is stuck in my head.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So wait, the "Destroyers" are a modern army that attacks from underground? What?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I wish I could give you all of my +1's.

Lord Blackwood, GENTLEMAN ADVENTURER, is indeed a swell enough chap.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The chances of anything coming from the Realm of Darkness are a million to one….

but still, they come!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Suddenly he got a call from the O5 Council Queen of England;

I didn't know there were cellphones around in the 19th century. Maybe

Suddenly he got a call from the O5 Council a messenger bearing the Queen of England's coat-of-arms* appeared and proclaimed;

or something like that would work better. Assuming you care about accuracy**. :)

*or whatever identifying scheme they use.
**or that I got it right enough myself. Really, am I off by much on the message-sending practices of the times?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You win an alien version of the Internet.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No offense taken. It's a very old trope. And for the most part, Bioware did it very well quite recently, so it is no surprise that people see Reapers.

If you want to know one of the key inspirations writing 1050, I direct you to this Stargate SG-1 episode. (Of course, the wiki article doesn't do it justice, and I recommend actually watching it to understand why, but there are shout-outs in the 1050 article.)

Also, funny you should mention Stargate Atlantis. When I write Rex, or perform him when I lend my voice talent to him for audio, I have David Hewlett's voice running through my head. Fun character, good actor, excellent inspiration.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You know, I have never played the Mass Effect trilogy, only ever hearing about it's plot through secondary media like X-play and Attack of the Show, and even I got the feeling they were talking about reapers. No offense to Hornby, it's still an excellent article.

Edit: And after a bit of thought, they also remind me of the Wraith from Stargate: Atlantis.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: ….Can't reply, mind blown to atoms.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
CHALLENGE ACCEPTED!

Suddenly, Dr. Rex Watson panicked as the Destroyers' UFO landed on his house.

"PUNY HUMANS!" said one of the destroyers "WE ARE HERE TO VAPORIZE MOST OF YOU AND SEND THE REST TO OUR SPICE MINES ON RIGEL V BARSOOM!"

Dr. Rex Watson tried to say "Oh no! I totally thought this wasn't going to happen! How dramatically ironic!" but he couldn't because he had just been vaporized by the destroyers' space guns.

They then shot Delivery Agent Roadrunner Professor Moriarty with a teleportation gun that sent him to their spice mines on Rigel V Barsoom, where he lived for 70 tortuous years solar sweeps before he was eaten by a sand worm cannibals.

Dr. Edison Lord Blackwood meanwhile was in Tokyo, dueling a ninja master for the possession of a new SCP artifact for his collection; a magic sword that can shoot lighting and fly and sing and show one's true self that can't cut the innocent and also causes demons to explode that was forged by an ancient samurai out of 666666 thousand year old Egyptian sarcophagi. Suddenly he got a call from the O5 Council Queen of England; "Edison Lord Blackwood, The Destroyers have come to vaporize most of us and send the rest to their spice mines on Rigel V Barsoom! Are you a bad enough dude swell enough chap to blast aliens?"

"Wot wot? Am I a 10-dan Akido master in addition to an ace fighter pilot and a master sharpshooter?" said Edison Blackwood, effortlessly parrying each one of the ninja's strikes. "Fuck yeah I'm a bad enough dude I am indeed a swell enough chap if I do say so myself. In fact you could say that I'm a 12.0 on the 10.0 scale of badness I am, without hyperbolie, the most swell chap in all of england! " He then sliced the ninja master in half using a single stroke. "I'll be there in five minutes! Pip pip cherrio! For queen and country!"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is great. I wish we had more "alternate take on good SCPs" tales.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Doh! Edited.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Rex could be wrong, and all the conjectures in the SCP-1050 article could be absolutely right. Or all the conjectures in the SCP-1050 could be absolutely right, and Rex could be wrong.

Did you mean for there not to be a "Rex is right" option?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Debunk" is such a strong word… this is just an alternate interpretation. One of the things I liked about writing SCP-1050 is there's a lot we know about it, but there's also a helluva lot we don't know and can only guess about. And there's no clear way for the Foundation to fill in the gaps in its knowledge (which, when you think about it, would probably terrify it as much as or more as whatever the threat turned out to be).

Rex could be right, and all the conjectures in the SCP-1050 article could be absolutely wrong.

Or all the conjectures in the SCP-1050 could be absolutely right, and Rex could be wrong.

It's up to you and your headcanon to decide. We won't know for certain until [DATA EXPUNGED BY O5 COMMAND] when the 'Destroyers' either do, or do not, show up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wait, did Hornby just debunk his own article?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Coming Soon: Lord Blackwood vs. The Destroyers
How a Sea Slug saved the planet from a fate worse than death!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
And now for the unoffical sequel:

Suddenly, Dr. Rex panicked as the Destroyers' UFO landed on his house.

"PUNY HUMANS!" said one of the destroyers "WE ARE HERE TO VAPORIZE MOST OF YOU AND SEND THE REST TO OUR SPICE MINES ON RIGEL V!"

Dr. Rex tried to say "Oh no! I totally thought this wasn't going to happen! How dramatically ironic!" but he couldn't because he had just been vaporized by the destroyers' space guns.

They then shot Delivery Agent Roadrunner with a teleportation gun that sent him to their spice mines on Rigel V, where he lived for 70 tortuous years before he was eaten by a sand worm.

Dr. Edison meanwhile was in Tokyo, dueling a ninja master for the possession of a new SCP; a magic sword that can shoot lighting and fly and sing and show one's true self that can't cut the innocent and also causes demons to explode that was forged by an ancient samurai out of 666666 thousand year old Egyptian sarcophagi. Suddenly he got a call from the O5 Council; "Edison, The Destroyers have come to vaporize most of us and send the rest to their spice mines on Rigel V! Are you a bad enough dude to blast aliens?"

"Am I a 10-dan Akido master in addition to an ace fighter pilot and a master sharpshooter?" said Edison, effortlessly parrying each one of the ninja's strikes. "Fuck yeah I'm a bad enough dude. In fact you could say that I'm a 12.0 on the 10.0 scale of badness!" He then sliced the ninja master in half using a single stroke. "I'll be there in five minutes!"

To be continued in… DOCTOR EDISON: ALIEN BLASTER!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Mass Effect Rapers? Gosh that sounds horrible…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Rex Sponge's Revealing Revelations Part 2: The Wrath of Khan.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
They clearly can't be Reapers: there's no mention of some twerp god-child with aggravating circular logic.

Also, good story. +1'd

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Rex Sponge's Revealing Revelations: Rex Performs a Logistical Examination of Anomalous Object

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice. It is good to see some logical thinking and conclusions in a tale.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If you were going to argue that they aren't reapers, why did you quote Mass Effect in the tale?

Irony. And because I'm not arguing that they're not Reapers. I'm arguing they don't have to be Reapers.

Edit: Perhaps I should re-tag this as a "supplement" and parent it to 1050. I'm open to people's opinions on doing that, since I think it could go either way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If you were going to argue that they aren't reapers, why did you quote Mass Effect in the tale? I don't have a problem with 1050 referring to reapers, since it's a really neat SCP, but this tale does not do a thing for me, I'm afraid to say. It doesn't really go anywhere dramatically and I also don't like that the only scps the officers can think to mention are three of the most referenced of the whole site…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I still can't see them as anything but the Mass Effect *Reapers, the whole thing fits them so well (hide out in the 'realm of darkness' aka dark space, they seem to repeat a cycle of destruction, they're space monsters, the tablet can even be seen as a form of the Prothean beacon, etc.)

Nice tale though, it doesn't sway my mind but it's a good read.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
An alternative assessment of the alleged 'Destroyers' in SCP-1050.

Because I was tired of people automatically assuming they were knockoffs of the Mass Effect universe Reapers. They were not intended as such. While there is evidence to suggest that interpretation, it was my intent to make it possible to hold other views. Headcanon it as you wish, but here's an official alternative.
